transcript: Would you like some tea? Yes, thank you. Cookies? No, thank you. Did you read The Wasteland through to the end? Yeah, I understand it. That's why we're here. I read it four times. It bears repeated readings. You know, I felt like he was a professor showing off how smart he was. Oh, and then maybe he was drunk. I think T.S. Eliot was a rather sober man. When I read old poems, like the old English poems Mr. Hoffman assigns, they don't seem old. But when I read The Wasteland, it seems old to me. But it was written in the modern era. I don't think anybody's going to read it in a hundred years. Why not? I don't know. Look at this picture. It looks like an undertaker. I think a poem that won't be good in 100 years isn't good now.
